content: LiteLLM pitfalls: Usage-based routing adds significant latency from Redis operations not suitable for production; default cooldown (3 fails, 5s) cascades rapidly under bursty traffic; RPM/TPM must be explicitly set or router randomly picks deployment; Azure returns generic model names causing inaccurate cost tracking without base_model config; unknown models default to 1 dollar cost in lowest-cost routing; tpm/rpm placement differs between v1 and v2 configs causing silent ignoring; pre_call_checks must be explicitly enabled for context window filtering; latency routing without buffer sends all traffic to single fastest deployment.
